# Redis Patterns Applied

> The 30 Redis design patterns, taught applied — each shown where the BCS architecture uses it: EchoMQ backed by
> Valkey, Valkey under the hood, EchoStore in front, codemojex the worked consumer. Served at /redis-patterns. Nine chapters: R0 Overview + the
> pattern chapters R1–R8. Each pattern is shown applied in a real system; the worked examples are real keys,
> commands, and atomic Lua scripts, quoted verbatim from committed records. A `soon` marker means the chapter or
> module is specified and on the way.

## Overview

- [R0 · Overview](/redis-patterns/overview): where Valkey sits in codemojex — the EchoMQ bus and the EchoStore near-cache over one Valkey — the reading protocol for a pattern, and the door to R1–R8.
  - [R0.2 · Valkey under codemojex](/redis-patterns/overview/redis-under-game): the facade seam, the two roles Valkey plays, the multi-runtime tier.
  - [R0.3 · Patterns become protocol](/redis-patterns/overview/patterns-become-protocol): the four-layer model and the owned, version-fenced core; the braced `emq:{q}:` data model as the cross-runtime contract.

## Chapters

- [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching): the read path — cache-aside, write-through, write-behind, client-side caching, stampede prevention, sessions; a workshop.
- [R2 · Coordination](/redis-patterns/coordination): atomic updates, distributed locking, redlock (contrast), cross-shard consistency, hash-tag co-location.
- [R3 · Reliable Queues](/redis-patterns/queues): the processing list, at-least-once, stalled recovery, the atomic state machine, blocking vs polling.
- [R4 · Time, Delay & Priority](/redis-patterns/time-delay-priority): delayed queue, schedulers, priority composite scores, backoff & retry, leaderboards.
- R5 · Streams & Events (soon): event sourcing, consumer patterns, pub/sub vs streams, projections.
- R6 · Flow Control (soon): rate limiting, priorities & fairness, groups, batches, worker concurrency.
- R7 · Data Modeling & Memory (soon): primary database, memory optimization, probabilistic (contrast), bitmaps, vectors, geospatial.
- R8 · Production & Operations (soon): kernel tuning, persistence/failover, Pinterest, Twitter/X, Uber, operating EchoMQ.

## Notes

- The grounding map (which pattern → which real EchoMQ/EchoStore artifact) is `docs/redis-patterns/redis-patterns.roadmap.md`; never invented.
- Companion courses: [/echomq](/echomq) is the EchoMQ protocol in depth; [/bcs](/bcs) is the Branded Component System these patterns run inside; [/elixir](/elixir) is the functional-Elixir craft behind the echo umbrella where the connector and the cache live.
